bead.title: Cache infrastructure: per-source disk cache + ParseModelRef + 10-test robustness matrix
bead.description:
Implement the per-source on-disk cache per ADR-012. New package
`internal/discoverycache/`. Foundation for M2 (snapshot assembler) and
M3 (runtime signals); no consumers in this bead.

Also includes the canonical model identity helpers (`ModelRef`,
`ParseModelRef`) since they're the cache's primary key.

## In-scope files

- internal/discoverycache/cache.go — public API (Get, ForceRefresh,
  WriteRefresh, Prune)
- internal/discoverycache/lock.go — mutation lock (extends
  acquireMatrixLock idiom; PID+atomic-create with crash recovery)
- internal/discoverycache/marker.go — refresh marker (PID+StartedAt+
  Deadline; staleness detection)
- internal/discoverycache/atomic_write.go — tmp+fsync+rename helper
- internal/discoverycache/singleflight.go — composes
  golang.org/x/sync/singleflight with file marker for cross-process
- internal/discoverycache/cache_test.go — basic single-process tests
- internal/discoverycache/multiprocess_test.go — the 10 mandatory
  tests using `TestHelperProcess` / child-spawn pattern
- internal/modelref/ref.go — canonical identity helpers
  (ModelRef struct, Parse, String, validation rules)
- internal/modelref/ref_test.go — table tests for parser

## Algorithms to implement (per ADR-012)

All 6 from ADR-012:
1. claim_refresh(source) → (ClaimedByMe | AlreadyInFlight(marker))
2. refresh_and_commit(source, dataBytes) — atomic rename + marker
   cleanup
3. wait_for_refresh(source, marker, maxWait) — polling
4. read(source) → (data, age, fresh_bool)
5. force_refresh(source, refresher func()) — sync
6. In-process singleflight composition — use
   golang.org/x/sync/singleflight, composes with file-marker

## Public API sketch

```go
type Cache struct {
    Root string  // e.g. ~/.cache/fizeau
}

type Source struct {
    Tier            string  // "discovery" or "runtime"
    Name            string  // e.g. "openrouter", "claude-subscription"
    TTL             time.Duration
    RefreshDeadline time.Duration
}

type ReadResult struct {
    Data       []byte
    Age        time.Duration
    Fresh      bool  // true if Age < TTL
    Stale      bool  // true if Age >= TTL
}

func (c *Cache) Read(s Source) (ReadResult, error)

// Refresher is the source-fetch function. Cache handles single-flight
// + atomic write + marker lifecycle.
type Refresher func(ctx context.Context) ([]byte, error)

func (c *Cache) Refresh(s Source, fn Refresher) error          // sync, force
func (c *Cache) MaybeRefresh(s Source, fn Refresher)           // async, no-op if fresh
func (c *Cache) Prune(activeSources []Source) error             // remove unknown
```

## Canonical identity (`internal/modelref`)

```go
type ModelRef struct {
    Provider string  // slug: lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens, dots
    ID       string  // provider's canonical model ID; case-preserved
}

func Parse(s string) (ModelRef, error)  // split on first /
func (r ModelRef) String() string         // <provider>/<id>
func (r ModelRef) Validate() error        // empty checks, char rules
```

Edge cases per ADR / discussion:
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b` → provider=openrouter, id=qwen/qwen3.6-27b
- `vidar-ds4/deepseek-v4-flash` → provider=vidar-ds4, id=deepseek-v4-flash
- Empty either side → error
- Provider name with slash → error
- Multiple slashes → split on first only
- Future M5: `@<sub_provider>` suffix is a separate concern, not in this bead

## Mandatory test matrix (per ADR-012)

ALL 10 tests in `multiprocess_test.go` MUST pass:

1. concurrent claim → exactly one ClaimedByMe
2. reader during refresh → atomic rename, no torn read
3. crash during refresh (kill -9 child) → next process claims via dead-PID
4. refresh timeout → marker deadline expires, claim
5. force-refresh waits + returns fresh
6. concurrent normal+force → singleflight composition
7. atomic rename: 100 readers + 1 writer × 100 versions, no torn read
8. cache prune doesn't race active sources
9. stale-while-revalidate: stale returns immediately + bg refresh fires
10. PID reuse safety: deadline check is the fallback

Use `TestHelperProcess` pattern (Go stdlib pattern; spawn `os.Args[0]
-test.run=TestHelperProcess --` with custom args).

## Out of scope

- M2 (snapshot assembler) — uses this cache.
- M3 (runtime signals) — uses this cache.
- M4 (CLI) — top-level surface.
- Any actual provider/harness fetch logic — Refresher is injected.

## Depends on

- ADR-012 (M0) accepted before this bead executes (uses its design
  contract).
bead.acceptance:
1. go test ./internal/discoverycache/... ./internal/modelref/... -count=1 passes and includes the 10 multiprocess behaviors named in the bead description.
2. go test ./internal/modelref/... -run "Parse|ModelRef" -count=1 passes with table coverage for split-on-first-slash, empty provider/id, invalid provider chars, case-preserved model IDs, and multi-slash model IDs.
3. go test ./internal/discoverycache/... -run "Test.*Contention|Test.*P99|Test.*Concurrent|Test.*Atomic|Test.*Stale" -count=1 passes, including a contention test that asserts Cache.Read p99 latency stays under 100ms while refresh/write activity is concurrent.
4. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
5. go list -deps ./internal/discoverycache/... | rg "flock|lockfile|gofrs|juju|nightlyone" returns no matches, proving no external lock-library dependency was introduced.
